text:
context7 MCP: 2 tools, 1,020 tokens.

ran the fix CLI.

before: F (7.5/100)
after: A (96.1/100)

the problem was documentation stuffed into schema fields. resolve-library-id had a 2,006-character description. after trim: 99 chars.

ran fix CLI on 8 F-grade MCP servers. same CLI, completely different outcomes.

Context7: F → A (−78% tokens)
Desktop Commander: F → C (−70% tokens)
Notion: F → D- (−10% tokens)

the gap is the type of F you start with.

two types of F-grade MCP server:

1. documentation in the wrong place (descriptions contain usage guides) → fix CLI can reach A
2. descriptions exist but are behavioral instructions → automated fix tops out at D-

which one is yours? {url}
